diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: SportsTeam. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: numberOfMembers - Example triple: (1. FC Köln | numberOfMembers | 50000)
Predicate: season - Example triple: (1. FC Köln | season | 2014)
Predicate: league - Example triple: (1. FC Magdeburg | league | 3. Liga)
Predicate: manager - Example triple: (1. FC Magdeburg | manager | Jens Härtel)
Predicate: ground - Example triple: (A.C. Cesena | ground | Cesena)
Predicate: fullName - Example triple: (A.C. Chievo Verona | fullName | "Associazione Calcio ChievoVerona S.r.l.")
Predicate: chairman - Example triple: (A.E Dimitra Efxeinoupolis | chairman | Antonis Milionis)
Predicate: location - Example triple: (A.E Dimitra Efxeinoupolis | location | Greece)
Predicate: owner - Example triple: (AFC Ajax | owner | AFC Ajax N.V.)
Predicate: nickname - Example triple: (AFC Ajax (amateurs) | nickname | "Lucky Ajax")
Predicate: chairmanTitle - Example triple: (Agremiação Sportiva Arapiraquense | chairmanTitle | "President")
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Akron, Ohio | isPartOf | Summit County, Ohio)
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Akron, Ohio | leader | Dan Horrigan)
Predicate: part - Example triple: (Amsterdam | part | Amsterdam-Noord)
Predicate: mayor - Example triple: (Athens | mayor | Giorgos Kaminis)
Predicate: champions - Example triple: (Azerbaijan Premier League | champions | Qarabağ FK)
Predicate: country - Example triple: (Campeonato Brasileiro Série C | country | Brazil)
Predicate: club - Example triple: (Christian Panucci | club | A.S. Livorno Calcio)
Predicate: capital - Example triple: (Greece | capital | Athens)
Predicate: currency - Example triple: (Greece | currency | Euro)
Predicate: demonym - Example triple: (Greece | demonym | Greeks)
Predicate: language - Example triple: (Greece | language | Greek language)
Predicate: leaderParty - Example triple: (Gubbio | leaderParty | Left Ecology Freedom)
Predicate: officialLanguage - Example triple: (Italy | officialLanguage | Italian language)
Predicate: birthPlace - Example triple: (James Pallotta | birthPlace | Boston)
Predicate: youthclub - Example triple: (Michele Marcolini | youthclub | Torino F.C.)
Predicate: operator - Example triple: (Sportpark De Toekomst | operator | AFC Ajax)
Predicate: tenant - Example triple: (Sportpark De Toekomst | tenant | Ajax Youth Academy)
Predicate: city - Example triple: (St. Vincent–St. Mary High School | city | Akron, Ohio)
Predicate: state - Example triple: (St. Vincent–St. Mary High School | state | Ohio)

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural‑language sentence from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build entity graph to track relationships
    entity_graph = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in entity_graph:
            entity_graph[t.subject] = []
        entity_graph[t.subject].append(t)

    # Find the main subject (entity that appears most as subject)
    subject_counts = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_counts[t.subject] = subject_counts.get(t.subject, 0) + 1

    if subject_counts:
        main_subject = max(subject_counts, key=subject_counts.get)
    else:
        main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Predicate to text mapping with better templates
    templates = {
        "numberOfMembers": "has {obj} members",
        "season": "during the {obj} season",
        "league": "in the {obj} league",
        "manager": "managed by {obj}",
        "ground": "plays at {obj}",
        "fullName": "whose full name is {obj}",
        "chairman": "chaired by {obj}",
        "location": "located in {obj}",
        "owner": "owned by {obj}",
        "nickname": "nicknamed {obj}",
        "chairmanTitle": "with chairman title {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "which is part of {obj}",
        "leader": "led by {obj}",
        "part": "includes {obj}",
        "mayor": "with mayor {obj}",
        "champions": "champions are {obj}",
        "country": "in {obj}",
        "club": "played for {obj}",
        "capital": "whose capital is {obj}",
        "currency": "whose currency is {obj}",
        "demonym": "whose people are called {obj}",
        "language": "where the language is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "whose leader belongs to {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "whose official language is {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "born in {obj}",
        "youthclub": "who started in youth club {obj}",
        "operator": "operated by {obj}",
        "tenant": "tenant {obj}",
        "city": "in the city of {obj}",
        "state": "in the state of {obj}",
    }

    # Build connected sentence using relative clauses
    processed = set()
    result_parts = []

    # Start with main subject
    if main_subject in entity_graph:
        main_triples = entity_graph[main_subject]
        main_attrs = []

        for t in main_triples:
            processed.add((t.subject, t.predicate, t.object))
            template = templates.get(t.predicate, "{subj} {pred} {obj}")

            if t.predicate == "league":
                main_attrs.append(f"plays in the {t.object} league")
            elif t.predicate == "season":
                main_attrs.append(f"during the {t.object} season")
            elif t.predicate == "ground":
                main_attrs.append(f"plays at {t.object}")
            elif t.predicate == "manager":
                main_attrs.append(f"managed by {t.object}")
            else:
                main_attrs.append(template.format(obj=t.object))

        # Build main clause
        if main_attrs:
            result_parts.append(f"{main_subject} {', '.join(main_attrs[:2])}" +
                              (" and " + main_attrs[2] if len(main_attrs) > 2 else ""))

    # Connect related entities through chained relationships
    for t in triples:
        if (t.subject, t.predicate, t.object) not in processed:
            # Check if object connects to main subject
            if t.object in entity_graph:
                sub_triples = entity_graph[t.object]
                sub_attrs = []

                for st in sub_triples:
                    sub_processed = (st.subject, st.predicate, st.object)
                    if sub_processed not in processed:
                        processed.add(sub_processed)
                        template = templates.get(st.predicate, "{obj}")
                        sub_attrs.append(template.format(obj=st.object))

                if sub_attrs:
                    result_parts.append(f"{t.object} {', '.join(sub_attrs)}")

    return ", ".join(result_parts) + "." if result_parts else ""

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

IMPORTANT: Do not rewrite the entire program - focus on targeted improvements.

=======
def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural‑language sentence from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build entity graph to track relationships
    entity_graph = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in entity_graph:
            entity_graph[t.subject] = []
        entity_graph[t.subject].append(t)

    # Find the main subject (entity that appears most as subject)
    subject_counts = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_counts[t.subject] = subject_counts.get(t.subject, 0) + 1

    if subject_counts:
        main_subject = max(subject_counts, key=subject_counts.get)
    else:
        main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Predicate to text mapping with better templates
    templates = {
        "numberOfMembers": "has {obj} members",
        "season": "during the {obj} season",
        "league": "in the {obj} league",
        "manager": "managed by {obj}",
        "ground": "plays at {obj}",
        "fullName": "whose full name is {obj}",
        "chairman": "chaired by {obj}",
        "location": "located in {obj}",
        "owner": "owned by {obj}",
        "nickname": "nicknamed {obj}",
        "chairmanTitle": "with chairman title {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "which is part of {obj}",
        "leader": "led by {obj}",
        "part": "includes {obj}",
        "mayor": "with mayor {obj}",
        "champions": "whose champions are {obj}",
        "country": "in {obj}",
        "club": "played for {obj}",
        "capital": "whose capital is {obj}",
        "currency": "whose currency is {obj}",
        "demonym": "whose people are called {obj}",
        "language": "where the language is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "whose leader belongs to {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "whose official language is {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "born in {obj}",
        "youthclub": "who started in youth club {obj}",
        "operator": "operated by {obj}",
        "tenant": "tenant {obj}",
        "city": "in the city of {obj}",
        "state": "in the state of {obj}",
    }

    # Build connected sentence using relative clauses
    processed = set()
    main_attrs = []
    related_clauses = []

    # Process main subject triples
    if main_subject in entity_graph:
        main_triples = entity_graph[main_subject]
        
        for t in main_triples:
            processed.add((t.subject, t.predicate, t.object))
            
            if t.predicate == "league":
                main_attrs.append(f"plays in the {t.object} league")
            elif t.predicate == "season":
                main_attrs.append(f"during the {t.object} season")
            elif t.predicate == "ground":
                main_attrs.append(f"plays at {t.object}")
            elif t.predicate == "manager":
                main_attrs.append(f"managed by {t.object}")
            elif t.predicate == "numberOfMembers":
                main_attrs.append(f"has {t.object} members")
            elif t.predicate == "nickname":
                main_attrs.append(f"nicknamed {t.object}")
            else:
                template = templates.get(t.predicate, "{obj}")
                main_attrs.append(template.format(obj=t.object))

    # Build main clause
    main_clause = f"{main_subject}"
    if main_attrs:
        if len(main_attrs) == 1:
            main_clause += f" {main_attrs[0]}"
        elif len(main_attrs) == 2:
            main_clause += f" {main_attrs[0]}, {main_attrs[1]}"
        else:
            main_clause += f" {main_attrs[0]}, {main_attrs[1]}, and {main_attrs[2]}"

    # Process related entities through chained relationships
    for t in triples:
        if (t.subject, t.predicate, t.object) not in processed:
            # Check if this entity is connected to something we know
            if t.object in entity_graph:
                sub_triples = entity_graph[t.object]
                sub_attrs = []

                for st in sub_triples:
                    sub_processed = (st.subject, st.predicate, st.object)
                    if sub_processed not in processed:
                        processed.add(sub_processed)
                        template = templates.get(st.predicate, "{obj}")
                        sub_attrs.append(template.format(obj=st.object))

                if sub_attrs:
                    if len(sub_attrs) == 1:
                        related_clauses.append(f"which is {t.predicate} {t.object}, {sub_attrs[0]}")
                    else:
                        related_clauses.append(f"which is {t.predicate} {t.object} and {', '.join(sub_attrs)}")
            elif t.subject in entity_graph:
                # Process remaining triples from this subject
                sub_triples = entity_graph.get(t.subject, [])
                sub_attrs = []
                for st in sub_triples:
                    sub_processed = (st.subject, st.predicate, st.object)
                    if sub_processed not in processed:
                        processed.add(sub_processed)
                        template = templates.get(st.predicate, "{obj}")
                        sub_attrs.append(template.format(obj=st.object))
                if sub_attrs:
                    related_clauses.append(f"whose {t.predicate} is {t.object}, {sub_attrs[0]}")

    # Combine all into one coherent sentence
    if related_clauses:
        result = main_clause + " " + ", which ".join(related_clauses) + "."
    else:
        result = main_clause + "."

    return result
